current_run.description: This problem involves finding a secret N-bit integer x_s by exploiting a function that combines bitwise cyclic shifts and addition modulo 2^N. The function f(x) = ( (x <<< 1) + (x >>> 1) ) mod 2^N, where <<< is left cyclic shift and >>> is right cyclic shift. We aim to find x_s such that f(x_s) = secret_output, where secret_output is a predefined value. This is a non-linear function due to the cyclic shift operations.
